# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Update package list and install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip curl wget git vim && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set environment variables for Python
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Install additional development tools
RUN pip3 install --upgrade pip && \
    pip3 install numpy pandas matplotlib seaborn scikit-learn jupyterlab

# Copy the application code to the container
COPY . /app

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Expose port for JupyterLab (optional)
EXPOSE 8888

# Command to run JupyterLab (optional)
CMD ["jupyter", "lab", "--ip=0.0.0.0", "--no-browser", "--allow-root"]
